regime_justification: USDJPY hovering 18 pips below 160 intervention line, JGB yields at 29-year highs, BOJ June hike speculation mounting, but equity markets orderly and VIX subdued.

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY at 159.01, 18 pips below MOF watch line; Katayama pledged 'bold action as needed' at G7; JGB 10Y at 2.78%, highest since 1996; intervention risk elevated.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: USDJPY pinned near 160; BOJ June meeting June 15-16 (26 days out); Morgan Stanley warns hike failure triggers 170 or 140 JPY; applied unwind prior.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Countdown to BOJ June decision; JGB yields at cycle highs; spread still 187 bps but carry-to-vol deteriorating as vol may spike pre-meeting.

3w.label: Likely
3w.driver: BOJ June 15-16 decision window inside 3w horizon; market pricing June hike but risk of disappointment; intervention or surprise could cascade.

4w.label: Watchful
4w.driver: Post-BOJ clarity likely by week 4; if BOJ hikes, unwind risk recedes; if BOJ holds, USDJPY breaks higher and MOF intervenes—coin toss.

bottom_line: Regime Pre-Intervention (Stage 2): USDJPY 18 pips below 160, JGBs at 29yr highs, Katayama promises 'bold action,' BOJ June decision 26 days out—market coiled. Grades C/C/B/C: 1-2w elevated but stable absent catalyst; 3w risk spikes into BOJ meeting June 15-16. Key: if BOJ fails to hike, USDJPY breaks 160+ and MOF intervenes; if BOJ hikes, unwind pressure recedes but JGB/cross-asset volatility persists. Oil at $104.52, BTC $76,794 (down 0.2% 1d), VIX 18.06, MOVE 85.32—vol still suppressed but setup fragile. Watch: BOJ rhetoric next 2 weeks, any MOF rate-check rumors, USDJPY 1M risk reversals (data unavailable today).

stack.note: 18 pips below 160 MOF watch line; 7 consecutive sessions of decline per TE
Slow grind down; MOF historically reacts to pace not level—current pace subdued

Spread stable but JGBs rising faster than UST recently; carry intact but compressing
Elevated; Hormuz still closed; Trump suspended Tuesday strike but negotiations fragile

Vol still subdued despite geopolitical risk; complacency signal
Rates vol low despite UST at 16-mo highs and JGBs at cycle highs—calm before storm?

Range-bound; crypto liquidity neutral; no decoupling from risk-on yet

trigger_map.note: USDJPY 18 pips below 160; Katayama said 'bold action as needed' at G7 May 20; rate-check rumors possible any day.
Market pricing ~70% chance of June hike to 1.0%; if BOJ holds, USDJPY breaks higher; if hikes, short squeeze JPY rally triggers carry unwind.
Currently 2.78%, up from 2.75% yesterday; 3.0% would signal fiscal stress and BOJ policy failure—29yr highs already flashing warning.
Currently 85.32; rates vol still suppressed despite JGB/UST at extremes—latent but could flip fast if BOJ disappoints.

trigger_map.trigger: Energy / Hormuz shock pushing ¥/bbl > prior peak
trigger_map.note: ¥/bbl at ¥16,625; prior peak ~¥17,000 in late April; Hormuz closed, Trump suspended strike but talks fragile—risk persists.

trigger_map.trigger: USDJPY 1M risk reversals flipping to JPY-call premium
trigger_map.note: Data unavailable today; last check showed subdued skew; flip would confirm market pricing intervention/unwind—key to monitor.

narrative.p1: Tape quiet overnight: USDJPY drifted 4 bps lower to 159.01, now 18 pips below the 160 MOF intervention line. Finance Minister Katayama spoke at the G7 in Paris, saying Japan has 'understanding' from counterparts and will 'take bold action as needed'—clearest intervention warning yet. JGBs eased 2 bps to 2.78% but remain at 29-year highs after Monday's spike on strong GDP data (Q1 +0.5% QoQ). BOJ March minutes released, showing multiple members urged rate hikes 'without long intervals' if Iran war energy shock persists. Oil flat at $104.52; Trump suspended planned strike on Iran after Gulf allies intervened, but Hormuz remains closed and talks fragile. Nikkei down 1.2% to 59,804; orderly selloff from last week's 63,799 peak. VIX +1.3% to 18.06, MOVE -0.9% to 85.32—vol still compressed despite macro at extremes. No stage change from yesterday's yellow/Stage 2; setup unchanged but clock ticking toward BOJ June 15-16.
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP spread 186 bps (stable); USDJPY 1M implied vol unavailable (critical gap—last read ~7.6% in October 2025 per FX.com archive, likely higher now but can't confirm). Carry-to-vol proxy cannot be computed without current vol. USDJPY 5d pace -0.23%—slow grind, not the sharp pace that triggers MOF. Distance from 60d mean: USDJPY trading ~2% above 60d SMA (neutral to slightly stretched). Stage 2 justified: USDJPY at watch line, Katayama signaling, JGBs at cycle highs, BOJ June decision 26 days out—classic pre-intervention setup. Grades: Applied unwind prior to 1w and 2w; data alone would warrant D (low vol, stable spreads, no panic), but proximity to 160, BOJ June decision inside 3w window, and Katayama rhetoric elevate to C. 3w graded B on BOJ binary: if no hike, USDJPY breaks 160+ and MOF acts (cascade risk); if hike, JPY short squeeze unwinds carry (also cascade risk). 4w back to C assuming clarity post-BOJ. Key missing datapoint: USDJPY 1M 25Δ risk reversals—would show if market pricing intervention; unavailable today.
narrative.p3: Crypto liquidity: BTC at $76,794, down 0.2% on the day, range-bound between $76k-$78k for past week. No decoupling from equities yet; BTC tracking risk-on/off but without conviction. XRP data not captured in detail; BTC open interest and BTC-DXY correlation unavailable in search results. Read: crypto is a passenger, not a driver—if USDJPY unwinds and equities sell off, BTC drains as leveraged positions liquidate. If BOJ hikes and yen rallies sharply, BTC likely falls 5-10% in sympathy with Nikkei and global risk assets. Conversely, if BOJ holds and USDJPY breaks higher (carry-on), BTC may rally on renewed liquidity optimism—but that scenario also raises intervention risk, capping upside. Net: crypto positioning should be defensive into BOJ June decision; any rally is a fade until yen carry thesis resolves.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: 1w and 2w both grade C because USDJPY is pinned 18 pips below 160 with Katayama on record promising intervention, yet no catalyst inside 14 days to force the issue. BOJ June meeting is 26 days out (June 15-16), so the binary event sits just outside the 2w window. Spreads stable (186 bps), VIX/MOVE subdued, Nikkei orderly. The setup is fragile—any headline (Iran escalation, MOF rate-check leak, BOJ board member hawkish speech) could trigger—but absent that, the most likely path is sideways churn with USDJPY testing 159.50-160.00 range. Applied unwind prior to move from D to C; pure data would argue 'unlikely' but the tactical setup is too coiled to ignore.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Week 3 jumps to B because BOJ decision June 15-16 falls inside this window. Market is pricing ~70% chance of hike to 1.0% (per Morgan Stanley/Bloomberg commentary). The binary is stark: (a) BOJ hikes → JPY rallies sharply (140 target per Morgan Stanley), carry positions unwind, Nikkei sells off, cross-asset vol spikes; (b) BOJ holds → USDJPY breaks 160+, MOF intervenes (possibly multi-step), also triggers carry unwind but from intervention shock rather than policy shock. Either path materially raises unwind probability. Grade B reflects this binary catalyst risk, not a prediction of which path.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Week 4 steps back to C because by then the BOJ decision is in the rearview and the market has priced the outcome. If BOJ hiked, the initial unwind may have run its course and stabilization begins (assuming no secondary shocks). If BOJ held and MOF intervened, week 4 is post-intervention stabilization phase. The structural carry trade pressure persists (oil high, JGBs at cycle highs, Fed not cutting), but the acute binary catalyst has resolved. Grade C reflects elevated medium-term risk but lower imminent probability than week 3.

watch_list: BOJ board member speeches or media interviews next 10 days—any hawkish/dovish signals will move USDJPY and June hike pricing.
USDJPY 1M 25Δ risk reversals—key gauge of intervention expectations; flip to JPY-call premium would confirm market pricing unwind.
MOF rate-check rumors or Katayama follow-up comments—any sign of operational prep for intervention.
Iran-US talks progress or collapse—Hormuz reopening would ease oil/inflation pressure and reduce BOJ hike urgency; escalation does opposite.
JGB 10Y breaking 3.0%—would signal fiscal stress and force BOJ to choose between YCC defense and rate hikes (both are JPY-positive but for different reasons).
Nikkei breaking below 58,000—would indicate risk-off acceleration and possible early unwind signature.
